senses.definition: a large early electronic device used in the 1940s and 1950s to separate isotopes by magnetic fields (an isotope separator).
examples: The scientists operated a calutron to enrich uranium during the Manhattan Project.
Calutrons were later replaced by more modern centrifuge methods.

senses.definition: plural of calutron; multiple devices used for isotope separation by magnetic fields.
examples: During the project, several calutrons were built and calibrated.
Calutrons contributed to the technology of uranium enrichment.

senses.definition: a type of French apple brandy distilled from cider; a liqueur from Normandy.
examples: He sipped Calvados after the meal as a digestif.
The bartender offered Calvados as a regional specialty.

senses.definition: plural form of calvados; multiple varieties or bottles of Calvados (the brandy).
examples: The shop offered several Calvadoses from different producers.
Tasting Calvadoses revealed subtle apple flavors.

senses.definition: a crucifix or shrine along a road; a scene of suffering, or a difficult ordeal.
examples: The calvaire stood by the roadside, carved from stone.
He described his long journey as a calvaire.

senses.definition: anatomical term for the upper part of the skull that encloses the brain.
examples: The calvaria consists of the fused skull bones.
In archaeology, tips about calvaria help identify species.

senses.definition: plural genitive or plural nominative of calvaria in anatomy/Latin usage.
examples: The specimens' calvariae were preserved for study.
In the text, calvariae describe the skull bones.

senses.definition: relating to the calvaria (upper skull bones).
examples: Calvarial bones fuse early in development.
Calvarial defects can affect brain protection.

senses.definition: pertaining to the calvaria; relating to the skullcap.
examples: Calvarian features include the dome-shaped skull cap.
Researchers studied calvarian structure in fossils.

senses.definition: plural of calvaria; multiple skullcaps or skull upper bones.
examples: Fossils showed varied calvarias among species.
The museum displayed several calvarias from ancient animals.

senses.definition: plural of calvary or calvary/mythological meaning; a place or concept of suffering; also a form of the plural of calvary (religious term).
examples: The path to the hill is dotted with calvaries along the way.
Artists painted many calvaries depicting the crucifixion.

senses.definition: the upper part of the skull; the skullcap.
examples: The calvarium was carefully measured in the forensic study.
Preserved calvarium specimens helped identify species.

senses.definition: plural of calvarium; multiple skullcaps.
examples: The exhibit included several calvariums from different species.
Researchers compared calvariums to study evolutionary changes.

senses.definition: a hill where Jesus Christ was crucified; a place or moment of great suffering; a punishing experience.
examples: Christians commemorate Calvary on Good Friday.
The long winter hike felt like Calvary to them.

senses.definition: a genus of fungi, including earthballs, with large spore-bearing structures.
examples: Calvatia species are known for their globe-like fruiting bodies.
Mycologists studied the Calvatia in the field.

senses.definition: to give birth to a calf; to produce a calf from a cow or other bovine animal.
examples: The dairy cow will calve in the spring.
She watched as the cow calved peacefully.

senses.definition: past tense of calve; produced a calf.
examples: The cow has calved successfully.
Last spring they calved two healthy calves.

senses.definition: a female calf or a cow that has recently calved; also used in some dialects to refer to a young cow.
examples: The calver stayed close to its mother.
Farmers labeled the calvers for milk production records.

senses.definition: past tense of calver; to have become a calver.
examples: The herd calvered last month.
Several cows calvered during the mild winter.

senses.definition: the act of a cow giving birth to a calf; a calving period.
examples: Calving season is busy for farmers during spring.
The farm tracks calvering to manage milk production.

senses.definition: plural of calver; female cows that have calved or are calving.
examples: The farm houses several calvers.
Calvers are important for steady milk supply.

senses.definition: young cows or bulls; immature bovines.
examples: The farmer feeds the calves every morning.
Two calves followed the mother around the field.
